summary: Small ftCommon DownBound matching fix that made ftCo_80097D40 fully match. The function stopped carrying its own duplicated x2228_b2 branch logic and dummy stack array, reused the shared static inlineA1 helper, then reset fp->mv.co.downspot.x4. The decomp.dev bot reported ftCo_80097D40 improved from 0.00% to 100.00% for +72 matched bytes, with a small .sdata2 improvement.
what_changed: Moved/placed static void inlineA1(Fighter_GObj* gobj) before ftCo_80097D40 so ftCo_80097D40 can call it directly.
inlineA1 contains the shared dispatch: GET_FIGHTER(gobj), check fp->x2228_b2, call ftCo_80097AF4(gobj) when true, otherwise call ftCo_8009794C(gobj).
ftCo_80097D40 now obtains Fighter* fp with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), calls inlineA1(gobj), then sets fp->mv.co.downspot.x4 = 0.
Removed the prior duplicated conditional body from ftCo_80097D40.
Removed the prior local dummy stack array u8 _[8] = { 0 } from ftCo_80097D40.
Kept the SOLUTION macro wrapping only ftCo_80097D40.
smart_moves: Reused an existing helper body instead of keeping duplicate branch logic in ftCo_80097D40, matching the apparent shared behavior with ftCo_80097D88.
Separated the common x2228_b2 dispatch from the ftCo_80097D40-specific downspot.x4 reset, which produced a cleaner source shape and a full match.
Removed artificial local padding once the helper-call structure produced the desired codegen.
decomp_lessons: When a target function duplicates the exact body of a nearby helper, try factoring or reusing the helper call before adding dummy stack locals.
A helper shared by adjacent functions can be the missing structure needed for matching, especially when one function adds only a small post-call side effect.
Prefer semantic source reshaping over opaque padding when the match can be achieved by aligning call structure and helper order.
Bot match reports are useful evidence: this PR specifically confirmed ftCo_80097D40 became 100% matched.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions in this file use the ftCo_ prefix, e.g. ftCo_80097D40, ftCo_80097AF4, ftCo_8009794C, ftCo_80097D88.
The local helper retained the temporary decomp-style name inlineA1; the slice gives no evidence of a more descriptive reviewed name.
Fighter object access in the final code uses GET_FIGHTER(gobj) for the edited functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Turned ftCo_80097D40 into a helper call plus one field store, rather than inlining/duplicating the branch body.
Removed a dummy stack allocation u8 _[8] = { 0 } that had been used in the old nonmatching version.
Changed the source expression from direct gobj->user_data access in ftCo_80097D40 to GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
The confirmed result was +72 bytes matched for ftCo_80097D40, 0.00% to 100.00%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev GALE01 bot report confirming one new match: ftCo_80097D40.
follow_up_queries: dummy stack u8 _[8] removed matching fix
